peripheral_notes.summary: Header pins with documented UART0 data functions; check the pin cautions before use.

notes: J2-1 board pad, silkscreen label FSD1. The stock board does not populate a header here.
Connected to the embedded flash memory in the ESP32-PICO-D4 SiP module.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-2 board pad, silkscreen label FSD3. The stock board does not populate a header here.
Connected to the embedded flash memory in the ESP32-PICO-D4 SiP module.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-3 board pad, silkscreen label FCLK. The stock board does not populate a header here.
Connected to the embedded flash memory in the ESP32-PICO-D4 SiP module.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-4 board header pin, silkscreen label IO21.
J2-5 board header pin, silkscreen label IO22.
J2-6 board header pin, silkscreen label IO19.
J2-7 board header pin, silkscreen label IO23.
J2-8 board header pin, silkscreen label IO18.
J2-9 board header pin, silkscreen label IO5.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls SDIO slave timing at reset.
J2-10 board header pin, silkscreen label IO10.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-11 board header pin, silkscreen label IO9.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J2-12 board header pin, silkscreen label RXD0.
Connected to the pin of the USB bridge chip on the board.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
J2-13 board header pin, silkscreen label TXD0.
Connected to the pin of the USB bridge chip on the board.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
ROM boot messages print to UART0 by default unless configured otherwise.
J2-14 board header pin, silkscreen label IO35.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J2-15 board header pin, silkscreen label IO34.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J2-16 board header pin, silkscreen label IO38.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J2-17 board header pin, silkscreen label IO37.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J3-1 board pad, silkscreen label FCS. The stock board does not populate a header here.
Connected to the embedded flash memory in the ESP32-PICO-D4 SiP module.
J3-2 board pad, silkscreen label FSD0. The stock board does not populate a header here.
Connected to the embedded flash memory in the ESP32-PICO-D4 SiP module.
J3-3 board pad, silkscreen label FSD2. The stock board does not populate a header here.
Connected to the embedded flash memory in the ESP32-PICO-D4 SiP module.
Used internally for communication between ESP32 and SPI flash memory. Avoid using this pin, as it may disrupt flash access.
J3-4 board header pin, silkscreen label FSVP.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J3-5 board header pin, silkscreen label FSVN.
Input-only GPIO. This pin does not support digital output or internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
J3-6 board header pin, silkscreen label IO25.
J3-7 board header pin, silkscreen label IO26.
J3-8 board header pin, silkscreen label IO32.
32.768 kHz crystal oscillator input function on this pin.
J3-9 board header pin, silkscreen label IO33.
32.768 kHz crystal oscillator output function on this pin.
J3-10 board header pin, silkscreen label IO27.
J3-11 board header pin, silkscreen label IO14.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J3-12 board header pin, silkscreen label IO12.
The operating voltage of ESP32-PICO-KIT's embedded SPI flash is 3.3 V. Therefore, MTDI should hold bit zero during module power-on reset.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
MTDI/GPIO12 controls VDD_SDIO voltage at reset unless overridden by eFuse settings.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J3-13 board header pin, silkscreen label IO13.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J3-14 board header pin, silkscreen label IO15.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls U0TXD printing and SDIO slave timing at reset.
JTAG debug signal; using it as regular GPIO can interfere with JTAG workflows.
J3-15 board header pin, silkscreen label IO2.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
Controls boot mode with GPIO0.
J3-16 board header pin, silkscreen label IO4.
J3-17 board header pin, silkscreen label IO0.
Connected to the board Boot button. Holding Boot and then pressing EN initiates firmware download mode.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. External circuits must not force the wrong boot-time level.
GPIO0=0 and GPIO2=0 selects Joint Download Boot mode.

general_warnings: Confirm the exact module variant before using header GPIOs reserved for on-module memory.
Review each listed caution before wiring a board-header GPIO.

power: Micro-USB port, 5V/GND header pins, or 3V3/GND header pins can power the board.
Use only one power supply option at a time; the official guide warns that multiple sources can damage the board or power source.
programming: USB-to-UART bridge (CP2102 in v4, CP2102N in v4.1) is used for flashing and serial debugging.
onboard_hardware: Boot button, EN button, 5 V power LED, USB-to-UART bridge, LDO regulator, and Micro-USB connector.
